summary: Full free-form sweep + tutorial walkthrough on dev-7ca39b6 against http://localhost:8080. The previous run's stop-idempotency regression is fixed (stop on an already-stopped or unknown run now returns {"stopped": true} exit 0). Two new fails surfaced: (1) `clier run capture --lines` silently accepts negative values (-1, -100) instead of rejecting out-of-range bounds the way `team list --page-size` does, (2) cross-coverage gap — the tutorial only walks the happy run path and does not exercise team create / update / reset-protocol / delete / star-unstar or any error envelope, even though every one of those is a first-class part of the discoverable surface. Everything else exercised cleanly: error envelopes are loud, multi-field violations surface together, JSON shape is stable with RFC3339 timestamps and `[]` for empty arrays, mutations are queryable through `team get` / `run view`, idempotency holds for star/unstar/run-stop, and disposable resources cleaned up with no orphans.

findings.title: clier --help enumerates the full subcommand surface
findings.rationale: Trust 1 — `clier --help` lists every top-level command (auth, open, run, team, tutorial, version) and each subcommand's --help recurses into the next layer (run start/list/view/capture/tell/stop/attach, team list/get/create/update/delete/star/unstar/reset-protocol). Nothing was reachable that wasn't documented, and nothing in the help text was a dead reference.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
Available Commands:
  auth           Log in and manage credentials
  open           Open clier surfaces in your browser
  run            Start, message, and stop tmux runs
  team           Browse and manage teams on the server
  tutorial       Walk through your first multi-agent run
  version        Print the build identity (binary, channel, server URL)

findings.title: First-run pointer to clier tutorial is present in top-level help
findings.rationale: Trust 1 — `clier --help` opens with `First run:  clier tutorial` so a fresh agent can orient with zero prior knowledge.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
First run:  clier tutorial
Per command: clier <command> --help

findings.title: Tutorial step 1 (clier team list) matches behaviour
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — running the literal command from the tutorial returned the catalog including jakeraft/hello-clier as the demo team, exactly as the tutorial promises.

findings.title: Tutorial step 2 (clier run start jakeraft/hello-clier) mints a run
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — `run start` printed a run_id, run_dir, session_name, and one entry per agent (hello-clier + hello-codex subteam) — the tutorial says to note the run_id and that's what the JSON exposes.

findings.title: Tutorial step 3 (heredoc tell) accepted
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — the heredoc form shown in the tutorial (`clier run tell --run <id> --to jakeraft.hello-clier <<'EOF' ... EOF`) was accepted and persisted; `run view` later showed the message in the messages array.
findings.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-082648-f7ec437f --to jakeraft.hello-clier <<'EOF' Greet your peer ... EOF

findings.title: Tutorial step 4 (clier run capture <run-id>) returns pane snapshots
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — `run capture` returned one entry per agent with agent_id / window / captured_at (RFC3339) / content; the tutorial promises 'JSON, no tmux attach required' and that's exactly the shape.

findings.title: Tutorial step 5 (clier run attach) skipped — needs interactive TTY
findings.skip_reason: Step 5 hands the terminal to tmux and requires an interactive TTY; the QA harness has no TTY. Verified the non-TTY refusal is loud and named (see claude.errors.run-attach-no-tty).
findings.rationale: Trust 2 — the tutorial itself notes this is the interactive step; behaviour is exercised indirectly via the non-TTY error path.

references.label: step 5
references.excerpt:
5. clier run attach <run-id>
   When you do want to watch live, attach. Detach with Ctrl-b d.

findings.title: Tutorial step 6 (clier run stop <run-id>) tears down cleanly
findings.rationale: Trust 2 + Trust 6 — `run stop` returned {"stopped": true}, the run dir under ~/.clier/runs/<run-id>/ was removed, and `run list` no longer surfaces the run. Exactly what the tutorial promises ('tmux session, clones, run.json — all gone').

findings.title: Unknown commands surface a single error: line and exit non-zero
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — both top-level and subcommand-level unknown commands print `error: unknown command "…" for "…"` on stderr and exit 1 (matches help-text promise of 'the same single-line shape across server validation, client-side validation, and unknown commands').

steps.label: unknown top-level
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "banana" for "clier"

steps.label: unknown subcommand
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "banana" for "clier team"

steps.label: extra positional after leaf
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "xxx" for "clier run list"

findings.title: team list rejects bad sort/agent-type/page-size/q/cursor with 422
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — every documented bound on `clier team list --help` is enforced by the server with a single `error: 422 Invalid argument: <field>: <detail>` line on stderr and exit 1.

steps.label: bad sort
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: sort: must be one of "stars_desc", "stars_asc", "updated_desc", "updated_asc"

steps.label: bad agent-type
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"

steps.label: page-size 0
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must be between 1 and 100

steps.label: page-size 101
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must not exceed 100

steps.label: q over 200 chars
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: q: exceeds 200 character cap

steps.label: bad cursor
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_token: invalid cursor

findings.title: team create surfaces every offending field in one envelope
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — when three independent fields are bad simultaneously the server reports them all in a single `error: 422 Invalid argument: …; …; …` line rather than short-circuiting on the first one.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"; command: must not be empty; git_repo_url: must be a GitHub HTTPS URL (https://github.com/owner/repo)

findings.title: team create surfaces every missing required flag in one line
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — running `team create` with no required flags lists all three missing flags client-side; passing one still surfaces the remaining two together.

steps.label: no required flags
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "agent-type", "command", "git-repo-url" not set

steps.label: one of three
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "command", "git-repo-url" not set

findings.title: team update rejects patches to immutable fields
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — namespace, name, and agent_type are immutable; patch-json attempts to change them return `error: 422 Invalid argument: <field>: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)`.

steps.label: agent_type
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)

steps.label: namespace
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: namespace: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)

steps.label: name
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: name: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)

findings.title: team update rejects null protocol with explicit alternative
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — `protocol: null` is rejected by the wire policy as documented in `team reset-protocol --help`; the server surfaces both alternatives in the single error line so the agent does not need to guess.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: protocol: must not be null (use "" for empty value, or :reset-protocol for default protocol)

findings.title: team update with no fields refuses client-side
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — empty patch is caught before the wire and the error line names every flag the user could have used.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: no fields to update — pass at least one --description / --command / --git-repo-url / --git-subpath / --subteam / --patch-json

findings.title: team delete refuses when team is referenced as a subteam
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — matches the documented rule in `team delete --help`. Deleting C-references-A then attempting to delete A returns 409 with named cause.
findings.command: clier team delete jakeraft/qa-20260507172301-claude-a   # while -c references it
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Failed precondition: team is referenced as a subteam by another team

findings.title: Owner-only verbs refuse on foreign namespaces with 403
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — create / update / reset-protocol / delete on someoneelse/* return `error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "someoneelse"` rather than leaking through to the server side.

steps.label: create
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "someoneelse"

steps.label: update
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "someoneelse"

steps.label: reset-protocol
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "someoneelse"

steps.label: delete
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "someoneelse"

findings.title: Reads/writes on unknown team slugs return 404
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — `team get/star/unstar/update/reset-protocol/delete` on an unknown slug all return a single `error: 404 Resource not found: team <ns>/<name> not found` line.

steps.label: get
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/no-such-team-qa not found

steps.label: star
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/no-such-team-qa not found

steps.label: unstar
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/no-such-team-qa not found

findings.title: run tell rejects missing flags / unknown agent / unknown run / empty body
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — every required flag is enforced client-side; an unknown --to or --run is rejected on lookup; empty body is rejected before send.

steps.label: no flags
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "run", "to" not set

steps.label: missing --to
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "to" not set

steps.label: empty body
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: message content is empty

steps.label: unknown agent
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: agent not found in run: jakeraft.no-such-agent

steps.label: unknown run
steps.command: clier run tell --run no-such-run --to jakeraft.hello-clier x
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

findings.title: run attach refuses without a TTY
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — non-interactive contexts get a single named error line and exit 1, matching the help-text promise that attach 'requires an interactive TTY'.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: attach requires an interactive terminal (stdin is not a TTY)

findings.title: run capture --lines silently accepts negative values
findings.fail_note: `clier run capture <run-id> --lines -1` and `--lines -100` exit 0 and return content of the same length as `--lines 0`. Trust 3 says out-of-range bounds must be loud and named — and `team list --page-size -1` does enforce this with `error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must be between 1 and 100`. Either negative `--lines` should be rejected with the same shape, or the help text should say negative is treated as 'visible area only' (currently it documents `0 = visible area only` and `--lines <n>` with no lower bound, leaving the agent to guess).
findings.rationale: The skill calls out 'out-of-range bounds' as a Trust 3 probe and the CLI inconsistently handles it across commands. `team list --page-size 0|-1` is rejected, `team list --page-size 101` is rejected, but `run capture --lines -1` is accepted silently — the agent has no way to discover the real range from --help.

steps.label: comparison: page-size enforces lower bound
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must be between 1 and 100

findings.title: run capture rejects non-numeric --lines and unknown --agent
findings.rationale: Trust 3 — non-numeric --lines is rejected by the flag parser and unknown --agent is rejected before the tmux send.

steps.label: non-numeric lines
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: invalid argument "abc" for "--lines" flag: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "abc": invalid syntax

steps.label: unknown agent
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: agent not found in run: jakeraft.no-such-agent

findings.title: team create → update → reset-protocol → delete is fully observable
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — every mutation is visible via `team get`. Created team A appears in `team list --namespace jakeraft`; `team update --description` reflects in the next `team get`; `team update --patch-json '{"protocol":"# Custom protocol\n"}'` then `team reset-protocol` round-trips the protocol field. After `team delete` the slug is gone from `team list`. Subteam clearing via `--subteam ""` returns `subteams: []` (not null).

steps.label: create A
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/qa-20260507172301-claude-a --agent-type claude --command "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions" --git-repo-url https://github.com/jakeraft/hello-clier --description "QA disposable team A"

steps.label: patch protocol then reset
steps.command: clier team update … --patch-json '{"protocol":"# Custom protocol\n"}'  &&  clier team reset-protocol …-a
steps.stdout_excerpt:
protocol: '# Custom protocol
' → reset starts with '# Team Protocol

You are **{{self}}'

steps.label: delete A & C
steps.command: clier team delete jakeraft/qa-…-c && clier team delete jakeraft/qa-…-a

findings.title: team star/unstar is idempotent and observable in team get
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — toggling stars updates `team get` (`star.starred` / `star.count`), and a second call is a no-op that prints the same JSON. Bad slug returns 404 (see claude.errors.team-not-found).

findings.title: run tell messages are visible via run view
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — every tell (positional, stdin heredoc, --from-attributed) showed up in `run view <id>` under messages with the right to/from/content fields, so the agent can audit what's been sent without poking tmux.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
messages count: 3
 to=jakeraft.hello-clier from=None content_first_line=Hello from QA — please ignore.
 to=jakeraft.hello-clier from=None content_first_line=QA test message:
 to=jakeraft.hello-clier from=jakeraft.hello-codex content_first_line=peer message attribution test

findings.title: Stopped runs are gone from run list
findings.rationale: Trust 4 + Trust 6 — after `run stop` the run is no longer in `run list`, and `run view` returns `error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs`. The wrapper dir under ~/.clier/runs/<run-id>/ is removed (ADR-0004 §4.6 honored).

steps.label: view post-stop
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

steps.label: fs check
steps.stderr_excerpt: ls: …/20260507-082538-4a6e3fef: No such file or directory

findings.title: run stop is idempotent on stopped and unknown ids (regression from prior run is fixed)
findings.rationale: Trust 4 — `clier run stop --help` claims 'Stop is final and idempotent: running it on an already-stopped run just clears any leftover dir.' Re-running stop on a just-stopped run, and stop on a run-id that never existed, both return `{"stopped": true}` exit 0. The previous QA run (qa-20260507-073601-517b0f7d) flagged this as a fail; on dev-7ca39b6 it now matches the help text.

findings.title: team list returns the documented data + meta envelope
findings.rationale: Trust 5 — top-level keys are exactly {data, meta}; meta has has_next (bool) and next_cursor (string, empty when no next page). Pagination via --page-size 1 + --page-token works and fields are stable across pages.

findings.title: Timestamps are RFC3339 across team and run reads
findings.rationale: Trust 5 — `team get` returns created_at/updated_at as RFC3339 with offset (`2026-05-07T15:41:28.428598+09:00`); `run capture` returns `captured_at` and `run view` returns `started_at` in the same shape. A regex check confirmed the RFC3339 shape on team timestamps.
findings.command: python3 -c 'rfc3339 ...; matches both timestamps' on clier team get jakeraft/hello-clier

findings.title: Empty collections serialize as [] not null
findings.rationale: Trust 5 — every observed empty-collection field across team and run reads is `[]`, not `null`: `subteams: []` on freshly created or cleared teams, `messages: []` on a fresh run before any tell, `data: []` is not observed (filters with no match still parse — n=3 baseline). Only optional scalars like message.from come back as null when unset, which is conventional.

findings.title: team list filters round-trip cleanly
findings.rationale: Trust 4/5 — namespace, agent-type, q (substring), and sort filters all return the expected subset. Verified via shape-stable JSON.

findings.title: Disposable team prefix has no orphans after the run
findings.rationale: Trust 6 — `team list --namespace jakeraft` filtered by the run's `qa-20260507172301-claude` prefix returns an empty match list, confirming every team minted during this QA run was deleted.
findings.command: clier team list --namespace jakeraft  # filter for qa-20260507172301-claude prefix

findings.title: run stop removes ~/.clier/runs/<run_id>/ entirely
findings.rationale: Trust 6 — covered in detail by claude.run.list-after-stop; calling out separately because ADR-0004 §4.6 names this guarantee. After both QA runs (free-form and tutorial) the wrapper dirs were gone.
findings.stderr_excerpt: ls: …/20260507-082538-4a6e3fef: No such file or directory ; ls: …/20260507-082648-f7ec437f: No such file or directory

findings.title: Tutorial does not exercise team-author / star / error-envelope surface
findings.fail_note: `clier tutorial` only walks the consume-a-public-team happy path (list → start → tell → capture → attach → stop). It does not cover team create / update / reset-protocol / delete / star / unstar — which are first-class subcommands surfaced in `clier team --help` and reachable from the same login — nor any error envelope (e.g. unknown command, 422 validation, 404 lookup, owner-only 403, immutable fields). For an agent that orients only from the tutorial, the second half of the surface is invisible. Free-form (this report) covers all of those dimensions; the tutorial does not.
findings.rationale: Cross-coverage check (§4) — free-form findings labelled claude.team.* and claude.errors.* describe behaviour the tutorial never exercises. The tutorial does mention `clier team create --help` under 'Going further', so the surface is *referenced*, but not *walked* — meaning fidelity-vs-behaviour cannot be checked the way steps 1–6 can.

references.label: Going further
references.excerpt:
Author your own team:    clier team create --help
Whole command surface:   clier --help

references.label: team --help
references.excerpt:
Available Commands:
  create / delete / get / list / reset-protocol / star / unstar / update
